<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Stochastic processes</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Filtrations 2 Discrete martingales 3 Optional stopping 4 Doob decomposition 5 Convergence 6 Brownian motion

Stochastic processes

Filtrations as a stream of expanding information; martingales as fair games whose conditional expectations don't drift; Doob's optional-stopping, decomposition, and convergence theorems as the three structural pillars; Brownian motion as the canonical continuous-time martingale with quadratic variation $[W]_t=t$. Every concept is paired with a small simulator you can run.

blocks.html:
1. Filtrations and adapted processes

Fix a probability space $(\Omega,\mathcal{F},\mathbb{P})$. A filtration is an increasing chain of sub-$\sigma$-algebras $$\mathcal{F}_0\subseteq\mathcal{F}_1\subseteq\mathcal{F}_2\subseteq\cdots\subseteq\mathcal{F}.$$ Read $\mathcal{F}_n$ as the information available at time $n$. Time advances: events that become decidable at later times can never be lost. The natural filtration of a process $(X_n)$ is $\mathcal{F}_n^X=\sigma(X_0,\ldots,X_n)$ — the smallest $\sigma$-algebra making every past observation measurable.

A stochastic process $(Y_n)_{n\ge 0}$ is adapted to $(\mathcal{F}_n)$ if $Y_n$ is $\mathcal{F}_n$-measurable for each $n$. Every process is adapted to its own natural filtration, by construction. Predictable is one step stricter: $(Y_n)$ is predictable if $Y_n$ is $\mathcal{F}_{n-1}$-measurable — known one step ahead of time.

Why filtrations? Conditional expectations $\mathbb{E}[\,\cdot\mid\mathcal{F}_n]$ live inside the filtration. The martingale identity $\mathbb{E}[M_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=M_n$ is meaningful only once we have nailed down what counts as "information at time $n$". Without the filtration, "fair game" has no formal content.

Stopping times. A random time $\tau\colon\Omega\to\{0,1,\ldots\}\cup\{\infty\}$ is a stopping time w.r.t. $(\mathcal{F}_n)$ if $$\{\tau\le n\}\in\mathcal{F}_n\quad\text{for every }n.$$ At time $n$, looking only at the data so far, you can decide whether stopping has happened. The first hitting time $\tau=\inf\{n:X_n\in B\}$ of a measurable set is always a stopping time; the last time the process was in $B$ before time $100$ is generally not — that requires looking at the future.

Worked example: hitting times of a random walk. Let $S_n$ be symmetric simple random walk on $\mathbb{Z}$ with $S_0=0$, and $\mathcal{F}_n=\sigma(S_0,\ldots,S_n)$. Then $\tau_a=\inf\{n:S_n=a\}$ is a stopping time: $\{\tau_a\le n\}=\bigcup_{k\le n}\{S_k=a\}\in\mathcal{F}_n$. The widget below lets you set bounds $a<0

<script>
/* §1 — filtration / stopping-time visualizer */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#filt-svg'), out=$('#filt-out');
  const aIn=$('#filt-a'), bIn=$('#filt-b'), aV=$('#filt-av'), bV=$('#filt-bv');
  const W=760, H=280, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  let path=[0], stopped=false, stopAt=null;
  function step(){
    if(stopped) return;
    const a=+aIn.value, b=+bIn.value;
    const last=path[path.length-1];
    const next = last + (Math.random()<0.5 ? -1 : +1);
    path.push(next);
    if(next<=a || next>=b){ stopped=true; stopAt=path.length-1; }
  }
  function reset(){ path=[0]; stopped=false; stopAt=null; draw(); }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    aV.textContent=aIn.value; bV.textContent=bIn.value;
    const a=+aIn.value, b=+bIn.value;
    const N=Math.max(40, path.length+5);
    const ymin=Math.min(a-1, ...path)-0.5;
    const ymax=Math.max(b+1, ...path)+0.5;
    const X = i => x0 + i/(N-1)*(x1-x0);
    const Y = v => y1 - (v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(y1-y0);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // boundary lines
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:Y(a),x2:x1,y2:Y(a),stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:Y(b),x2:x1,y2:Y(b),stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    const aT=SVG('text',{x:x1+4,y:Y(a)+4,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'}); aT.textContent='a='+a; svg.appendChild(aT);
    const bT=SVG('text',{x:x1+4,y:Y(b)+4,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--green)'}); bT.textContent='b='+b; svg.appendChild(bT);
    // zero
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:Y(0),x2:x1,y2:Y(0),stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'2 4'}));
    // path
    let d='';
    path.forEach((v,i)=>{
      d += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(i)+','+Y(v)+' ';
    });
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.6}));
    // dots
    path.forEach((v,i)=>{
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:X(i),cy:Y(v),r:2.5,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    });
    // current point ring
    const last=path.length-1;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:X(last),cy:Y(path[last]),r:6,
      fill:'none',stroke: stopped ? 'var(--cyan)' : 'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));
    // stop marker
    if(stopped){
      const xs=X(stopAt), ys=Y(path[stopAt]);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xs,y1:y0,x2:xs,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1,'stroke-dasharray':'3 3'}));
      const tt=SVG('text',{x:xs+4,y:y0+12,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
      tt.textContent='τ = '+stopAt; svg.appendChild(tt);
    }
    // y ticks
    for(let v=Math.ceil(ymin); v<=Math.floor(ymax); v++){
      const yp=Y(v);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0-4,y1:yp,x2:x0,y2:yp,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0-6,y:yp+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // x ticks
    for(let k=0;k<N;k+=Math.max(1,Math.floor(N/8))){
      const xp=X(k);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:y1,x2:xp,y2:y1+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=k; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // title
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent = `symmetric ±1 walk · 𝓕ₙ = σ(S₀,…,Sₙ) · τ = inf{n: Sₙ ∈ {a,b}}`;
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // readout
    const cur=path[last];
    out.textContent =
      `n = ${last}, S_n = ${cur}\n`+
      `boundaries: a = ${a} (lower, pink), b = ${b} (upper, green)\n`+
      (stopped
        ? `τ fired at n = ${stopAt}, S_τ = ${path[stopAt]}\nthe event {τ ≤ ${stopAt}} ∈ 𝓕_${stopAt} (a stopping time decision)`
        : `τ has not yet fired — {τ ≤ ${last}} is decidable from S₀,…,S_${last} alone`);
  }
  $('#filt-step').addEventListener('click',()=>{step();draw();});
  $('#filt-step10').addEventListener('click',()=>{for(let k=0;k<10;k++)step(); draw();});
  $('#filt-reset').addEventListener('click',reset);
  aIn.addEventListener('input',()=>{aV.textContent=aIn.value; draw();});
  bIn.addEventListener('input',()=>{bV.textContent=bIn.value; draw();});
  draw();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Discrete-time martingales

An adapted, $L^1$ process $(M_n)_{n\ge 0}$ is a martingale w.r.t.\ the filtration $(\mathcal{F}_n)$ if $$\mathbb{E}[M_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=M_n\qquad\text{for every }n\ge 0.$$ Replace "$=$" with "$\ge$" for a submartingale, "$\le$" for a supermartingale. The martingale identity says: given everything you know at time $n$, the best predictor of the next value is the current value. It is the formal expression of "fair game" — and equivalently, the increments $\Delta M_{n+1}=M_{n+1}-M_n$ have $\mathbb{E}[\Delta M_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=0$.

Examples.

  • Mean-zero random walk: $S_n=X_1+\cdots+X_n$ with $X_i$ i.i.d., $\mathbb{E}[X_i]=0$. Direct check: $\mathbb{E}[S_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=S_n+\mathbb{E}[X_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=S_n+0$.
  • Doob martingale: $M_n=\mathbb{E}[Z\mid\mathcal{F}_n]$ for any integrable $Z$. The tower property gives $\mathbb{E}[M_{n+1}\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=\mathbb{E}[\mathbb{E}[Z\mid\mathcal{F}_{n+1}]\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=\mathbb{E}[Z\mid\mathcal{F}_n]=M_n$. Every UI martingale is of this form for some terminal $Z=M_\infty$.
  • Squared walk minus drift: with $\mathrm{Var}(X_i)=\sigma^2$, $S_n^2-n\sigma^2$ is a martingale. Without the $-n\sigma^2$ correction, $S_n^2$ is only a submartingale.
  • Exponential martingale: for any $\theta\in\mathbb{R}$ such that $\varphi(\theta)=\mathbb{E}[e^{\theta X_1}]<\infty$, the process $M_n=e^{\theta S_n}/\varphi(\theta)^n$ is a martingale (Wald's exponential).

Worked example: a biased random walk and a martingale fix. Let $X_i\in\{\pm 1\}$ with $\mathbb{P}(X=+1)=p\ne 1/2$ and $q=1-p$. Then $\mathbb{E}[X_i]=p-q\ne 0$, so $S_n$ is not a martingale. But the centred process $S_n-n(p-q)$ is, and the multiplicative process $M_n=(q/p)^{S_n}$ is a martingale too — the latter is the engine of the gambler's-ruin formula in §3.

3. Optional stopping theorem

Doob's optional-stopping theorem. Let $(M_n)$ be a martingale and $\tau$ a stopping time. If at least one of the following holds:

  1. $\tau$ is bounded a.s.; or
  2. $M$ is uniformly integrable; or
  3. $\tau<\infty$ a.s.\ and the stopped process $(M_{n\wedge\tau})$ is uniformly bounded;
then $$\mathbb{E}[M_\tau]\;=\;\mathbb{E}[M_0].$$ The theorem is a martingale's defining promise made global: the fair-game expectation persists across optional stopping rules — provided the stopping is "honest", in any of the three precise senses above.

Optional-stopping fails without the hypotheses. Symmetric simple random walk $S_n$ stopped at $\tau=\inf\{n:S_n=1\}$ has $\tau<\infty$ almost surely and $\mathbb{E}[S_\tau]=1\ne 0=\mathbb{E}[S_0]$ — none of the three sufficient conditions hold ($\tau$ is a.s.\ finite but unbounded; $M$ is not UI; $(S_{n\wedge\tau})$ wanders to $-\infty$ unboundedly). Drop the hypotheses and the conclusion fails.

Worked example: gambler's ruin. Symmetric random walk on $\{a,a+1,\ldots,b\}$ with $a<0

Wald's identity follows the same template. For i.i.d. $X_i$ with mean $\mu$ and a stopping time $\tau$ with $\mathbb{E}\tau<\infty$, optional stopping on $S_n-n\mu$ gives $\mathbb{E}[S_\tau]=\mu\,\mathbb{E}[\tau]$. Both gambler's ruin and Wald are one-line corollaries of optional stopping applied to the right martingale.

/* §3 — gambler's ruin simulator */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#gr-svg'), out=$('#gr-out');
  const aIn=$('#gr-a'), bIn=$('#gr-b'), aV=$('#gr-av'), bV=$('#gr-bv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  let runs=[]; // {Stau, tau}
  function reset(){ runs=[]; draw(); }
  function simulate(N){
    const a=+aIn.value, b=+bIn.value;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      let s=0, n=0;
      while(s>a && s<b){ s += (Math.random()<0.5 ? -1 : +1); n++; if(n>20000) break; }
      runs.push({Stau:s, tau:n});
    }
    draw();
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const a=+aIn.value, b=+bIn.value;
    aV.textContent=`a = ${a}`; bV.textContent=`b = ${b}`;
    const pTheory = (-a)/(b-a);
    const eTheory = (-a)*b;
    const N=runs.length;
    let hitB=0, sumTau=0;
    runs.forEach(r=>{ if(r.Stau===b) hitB++; sumTau+=r.tau; });
    const pEmp = N>0 ? hitB/N : 0;
    const eEmp = N>0 ? sumTau/N : 0;
    // Title
    const cap=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:20,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    cap.textContent=`symmetric walk on {a,…,b}, S₀=0; martingale S_n gives P(S_τ=b)=−a/(b−a); martingale S_n²−n gives E[τ]=|a|·b`;
    svg.appendChild(cap);
    // Two bar plots side-by-side: hitting prob and expected time
    const cellW=300, cellH=180;
    const leftX=80, midX=420, topY=60;
    // -- hitting prob bar
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:leftX,y:topY,width:cellW,height:cellH,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // theory bar
    const thH = pTheory*cellH;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:leftX+30,y:topY+cellH-thH,width:90,height:thH,
      fill:'var(--violet)',opacity:0.7}));
    const thT=SVG('text',{x:leftX+75,y:topY+cellH-thH-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--violet)'});
    thT.textContent='theory '+pTheory.toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(thT);
    // empirical bar
    const empH = pEmp*cellH;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:leftX+170,y:topY+cellH-empH,width:90,height:empH,
      fill:'var(--yellow)',opacity:0.85}));
    const empT=SVG('text',{x:leftX+215,y:topY+cellH-empH-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    empT.textContent='empirical '+pEmp.toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(empT);
    // axis label
    const ll1=SVG('text',{x:leftX+cellW/2,y:topY+cellH+22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)','font-weight':600});
    ll1.textContent='P(S_τ = b)'; svg.appendChild(ll1);
    // -- expected time bar
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:midX,y:topY,width:cellW,height:cellH,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    const eMax=Math.max(eTheory, eEmp, 1);
    const ethH = (eTheory/eMax)*cellH;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:midX+30,y:topY+cellH-ethH,width:90,height:ethH,
      fill:'var(--violet)',opacity:0.7}));
    const eth=SVG('text',{x:midX+75,y:topY+cellH-ethH-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--violet)'});
    eth.textContent='theory '+eTheory.toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(eth);
    const eemH = (eEmp/eMax)*cellH;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:midX+170,y:topY+cellH-eemH,width:90,height:eemH,
      fill:'var(--cyan)',opacity:0.85}));
    const eem=SVG('text',{x:midX+215,y:topY+cellH-eemH-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    eem.textContent='empirical '+eEmp.toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(eem);
    const ll2=SVG('text',{x:midX+cellW/2,y:topY+cellH+22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)','font-weight':600});
    ll2.textContent='E[τ]'; svg.appendChild(ll2);
    // bottom note
    const nbl=SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:topY+cellH+50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    nbl.textContent = N>0 ? `${N} simulated runs` : 'click "simulate 1000" to populate';
    svg.appendChild(nbl);
    out.textContent =
      `a = ${a}, b = ${b}; runs = ${N}\n`+
      `theory: P(S_τ=b) = (−a)/(b−a) = ${(-a)}/${b-a} = ${pTheory.toFixed(4)}\n`+
      `theory: E[τ] = |a|·b = ${(-a)*b}\n`+
      (N>0
        ? `empirical: P(S_τ=b) ≈ ${pEmp.toFixed(4)}, E[τ] ≈ ${eEmp.toFixed(2)}`
        : `(no simulations yet)`);
  }
  aIn.addEventListener('input',draw);
  bIn.addEventListener('input',draw);
  $('#gr-run').addEventListener('click',()=>simulate(1000));
  $('#gr-reset').addEventListener('click',reset);
  draw();
})();
</script>

4. Doob decomposition and quadratic variation

Doob decomposition theorem. Every adapted $L^1$ process $(X_n)$ admits a unique decomposition $$X_n=X_0+M_n+A_n,$$ where $(M_n)$ is a martingale with $M_0=0$ and $(A_n)$ is predictable (i.e.\ $A_n$ is $\mathcal{F}_{n-1}$-measurable) with $A_0=0$. Existence is constructive: set $A_n=\sum_{k=1}^n\mathbb{E}[X_k-X_{k-1}\mid\mathcal{F}_{k-1}]$ and $M_n=X_n-X_0-A_n$. Uniqueness follows because two predictable martingales with $A_0=0$ must vanish (predictable + martingale $=$ constant).

The decomposition cleanly splits a process into "drift" ($A$, the predictable compensator) and "noise" ($M$, the unpredictable martingale increment). $X$ is a submartingale precisely when $A$ is non-decreasing; supermartingale when $A$ is non-increasing.

Predictable quadratic variation. For a square-integrable martingale $(M_n)$ with $M_0=0$, the squared process $M_n^2$ is a submartingale. Its Doob compensator is the predictable quadratic variation $$\langle M\rangle_n=\sum_{k=1}^n\mathbb{E}[(M_k-M_{k-1})^2\mid\mathcal{F}_{k-1}],$$ and $M_n^2-\langle M\rangle_n$ is a martingale. Heuristically: the variance of $M$ accumulates at the predictable rate $\langle M\rangle$.

Worked example: $S_n^2$ for symmetric simple random walk. With $X_i=\pm 1$ each w.p. $1/2$ and $S_n=X_1+\cdots+X_n$, the increments of $S_n^2$ are $$S_k^2-S_{k-1}^2=2S_{k-1}X_k+X_k^2.$$ Conditionally on $\mathcal{F}_{k-1}$, $\mathbb{E}[2S_{k-1}X_k\mid\mathcal{F}_{k-1}]=0$ and $\mathbb{E}[X_k^2\mid\mathcal{F}_{k-1}]=1$, so the predictable compensator is $A_n=n$. The martingale part is $M_n=S_n^2-n$ — exactly the example from §2. The discrete predictable quadratic variation is $\langle S\rangle_n=n$, presaging the continuous-time identity $[W]_t=t$ in §6.

<script>
/* §4 — Doob decomposition of S_n^2 = (S_n^2 - n) + n */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#doob-svg'), out=$('#doob-out');
  const nIn=$('#doob-N'), nV=$('#doob-Nv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  let paths=[];
  function genPath(N){
    const s=[0];
    for(let n=1;n<=N;n++) s.push(s[n-1] + (Math.random()<0.5 ? -1 : +1));
    return s;
  }
  function newOne(){ paths=[genPath(+nIn.value)]; draw(); }
  function many(){
    paths=[];
    const N=+nIn.value;
    for(let k=0;k<50;k++) paths.push(genPath(N));
    draw();
  }
  function reset(){ paths=[]; draw(); }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const N=+nIn.value;
    nV.textContent=`N = ${N}`;
    const ymin=-N*1.0, ymax=N*1.6;
    const X = n => x0 + n/N*(x1-x0);
    const Y = v => y1 - (v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(y1-y0);

5. Martingale convergence theorem

Doob's martingale convergence theorem. If $(M_n)$ is a martingale (or more generally a submartingale) with $$\sup_n\mathbb{E}|M_n|<\infty,$$ then there exists $M_\infty\in L^1$ with $M_n\to M_\infty$ almost surely. The proof goes through Doob's upcrossing inequality: $L^1$-boundedness limits the expected number of times $M_n$ crosses any interval $[a,b]$, so the sequence of values cannot oscillate forever.

$L^1$-boundedness is genuinely weaker than $L^1$-convergence. To upgrade a.s.\ convergence to $L^1$ convergence, we need uniform integrability: a family $\{M_n\}$ is UI if $$\lim_{K\to\infty}\sup_n\mathbb{E}\!\left[|M_n|\,\mathbf{1}_{|M_n|>K}\right]=0.$$ For a martingale, $L^1$-convergence is equivalent to UI. When $(M_n)$ is UI, $M_n\to M_\infty$ in $L^1$ as well as a.s., and $M_n=\mathbb{E}[M_\infty\mid\mathcal{F}_n]$ — every UI martingale is a Doob martingale of its terminal value.

$L^1$-bounded ≠ UI. Let $(X_i)$ be i.i.d. with $\mathbb{P}(X_i=2)=\mathbb{P}(X_i=0)=1/2$, and $M_n=\prod_{i=1}^n X_i$. Then $\mathbb{E}[M_n]=1$ for all $n$ (so the sup-norm is bounded), and $M_n\ge 0$. But the first time some $X_i=0$ is a.s.\ finite, after which $M_n=0$ forever. So $M_n\to 0$ a.s. — yet $\mathbb{E}[M_n]=1\not\to 0=\mathbb{E}[M_\infty]$. The convergence is a.s. but not in $L^1$, and the family is not UI.

Worked example: bounded martingales converge cleanly. Any martingale bounded above and below in absolute value is automatically UI (boundedness $\Rightarrow$ UI), so it converges both a.s.\ and in $L^1$. Doob martingales of bounded random variables ($Z\in L^\infty$) are always UI; sums of i.i.d.\ mean-zero variables truncated to a bounded set are not (they typically diverge). The widget contrasts a bounded martingale (converges to a definite limit) with the doubling product (converges a.s.\ to $0$ while keeping mean $1$).

<script>
/* §5 — bounded martingale vs. doubling product */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#conv-svg'), out=$('#conv-out');
  const mode=$('#conv-mode'), kIn=$('#conv-K'), kV=$('#conv-Kv');
  const W=760, H=320, x0=60, x1=W-30, y0=30, y1=H-40;
  const N=200;
  let paths=[];
  function genBounded(){
    // Polya urn: draw with prob = current red fraction; M_n = red fraction is a bounded martingale in [0,1]
    let red=1, total=2; // 1 red, 1 blue initially
    const seq=[red/total];
    for(let n=1;n<=N;n++){
      if(Math.random() < red/total){ red++; }
      total++;
      seq.push(red/total);
    }
    return seq;
  }
  function genDoubling(){
    const seq=[1];
    let v=1;
    for(let n=1;n<=N;n++){
      v = v * (Math.random()<0.5 ? 2 : 0);
      seq.push(v);
    }
    return seq;
  }
  function regen(){
    const K=+kIn.value;
    paths=[];
    for(let k=0;k<K;k++){
      paths.push(mode.value==='bounded' ? genBounded() : genDoubling());
    }
    draw();
  }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    kV.textContent=`K = ${kIn.value}`;
    const isBounded = mode.value==='bounded';
    const ymin = isBounded ? 0 : 0;
    const ymax = isBounded ? 1 : 4;
    const X = n => x0 + n/N*(x1-x0);
    const Y = v => y1 - (v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(y1-y0);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y1,x2:x1,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y1,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // mean line
    const meanLine = isBounded ? 0.5 : 1;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:Y(meanLine),x2:x1,y2:Y(meanLine),
      stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    // ticks
    const yTicks = isBounded ? [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1] : [0,1,2,3,4];
    for(const v of yTicks){
      const yp=Y(v); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0-4,y1:yp,x2:x0,y2:yp,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0-6,y:yp+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(const n of [0,50,100,150,200]){
      const xp=X(n); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:y1,x2:xp,y2:y1+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:y1+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=n; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // paths
    paths.forEach(p=>{
      let d='';
      p.forEach((v,n)=>{
        const yc = Math.min(ymax, Math.max(ymin, v));
        d += (n===0?'M':'L')+X(n)+','+Y(yc)+' ';
      });
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':0.9,opacity:0.6}));
    });
    // legend (viewport coords)
    const lg=SVG('text',{x:x1-10,y:y0+14,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--violet)'});
    lg.textContent= isBounded ? 'mean = 1/2 (theoretical)' : 'mean = 1 (martingale)';
    svg.appendChild(lg);
    // empirical at n=N
    const finals = paths.map(p=>p[N]);
    const m = finals.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0)/finals.length;
    const zeroFrac = finals.filter(v=>v===0).length/finals.length;
    if(isBounded){
      const sd = Math.sqrt(finals.reduce((a,b)=>a+(b-m)**2,0)/finals.length);
      out.textContent =
        `Pólya urn: M_n = (red urns)/(total urns) ∈ [0,1]\n`+
        `${paths.length} paths, N = ${N}\n`+
        `M_N empirical mean = ${m.toFixed(3)} (target 0.500 by symmetry); empirical std ≈ ${sd.toFixed(3)}\n`+
        `bounded ⇒ UI ⇒ converges in L¹ to a (random) limit M_∞ ∈ [0,1] with E[M_∞] = 1/2`;
    } else {
      out.textContent =
        `doubling product: X_i ∈ {0, 2}, M_n = ∏ X_i\n`+
        `${paths.length} paths, N = ${N}\n`+
        `M_N empirical mean = ${m.toFixed(3)} (theoretical mean = 1.000)\n`+
        `fraction of paths now at 0: ${(zeroFrac*100).toFixed(1)}% (a.s. all collapse as N→∞)\n`+
        `M_n → 0 a.s., but E[M_n] = 1 — so NOT in L¹; family is not UI`;
    }
  }
  mode.addEventListener('change',regen);
  kIn.addEventListener('input',regen);
  $('#conv-new').addEventListener('click',regen);
  regen();
})();

6. Continuous-time martingales and Brownian motion

The discrete theory passes to continuous time once we replace $\mathbb{N}$ by $[0,\infty)$ and demand path regularity (right-continuity with left limits). The canonical example is Brownian motion $W_t$: $W_0=0$, increments $W_t-W_s\sim\mathcal{N}(0,t-s)$ are independent, and paths are continuous a.s.

Brownian motion as the prototype martingale. Three explicit martingales sit on top of $W$:

  • $W_t$ itself, w.r.t.\ its natural filtration.
  • $W_t^2-t$ — the continuous analogue of $S_n^2-n$. Its predictable quadratic variation is $\langle W\rangle_t=t$.
  • The exponential martingale $\exp(\theta W_t-\theta^2 t/2)$, $\theta\in\mathbb{R}$ — engine of Girsanov's change of measure.

Quadratic variation. For a partition $0=t_0quadratic variation $[W]_t=t$ a.s. Compare this with the total variation: $\sum_k|W_{t_{k+1}}-W_{t_k}|\to+\infty$. Brownian paths are wiggly enough that their quadratic variation is finite and linear in $t$, but rough enough that their total variation diverges. This is what makes ordinary Riemann–Stieltjes integration $\int f\,dW$ fail and forces the Itô integral.

Burkholder–Davis–Gundy. For any continuous local martingale $M$ with $M_0=0$ and any $p\ge 1$, there are universal constants $c_p,C_p$ with $$c_p\,\mathbb{E}\!\left[[M]_t^{p/2}\right]\;\le\;\mathbb{E}\!\left[\sup_{s\le t}|M_s|^p\right]\;\le\;C_p\,\mathbb{E}\!\left[[M]_t^{p/2}\right].$$ BDG converts maximal-norm estimates of a martingale into estimates of its quadratic variation — the bridge to Itô calculus and SDE theory.

Worked example: Brownian quadratic variation. Simulate $W$ on $[0,1]$ with mesh $1/N$. Compute $Q_N=\sum_{k=0}^{N-1}(W_{(k+1)/N}-W_{k/N})^2$. As $N$ grows, $Q_N\to 1=t$ in probability — and the variance of the approximation is $\mathrm{Var}(Q_N)=2t^2/N$ by direct calculation (Gaussian fourth moments). The widget plots $Q_N$ as a running sum alongside the constant line at $t$, so you watch the path's quadratic variation accumulate.

<script>
/* §6 — Brownian motion + accumulating quadratic variation */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#bm-svg'), out=$('#bm-out');
  const nIn=$('#bm-N'), nV=$('#bm-Nv');
  const W=760, H=360;
  const padL=60, padR=30;
  // top: path; bottom: QV
  const topT=30, topB=180;
  const botT=210, botB=H-40;
  let paths=[];
  function genPath(N){
    const dt=1/N;
    const w=[0]; const qv=[0];
    for(let i=1;i<=N;i++){
      const dW = Math.sqrt(dt)*randn();
      w.push(w[i-1] + dW);
      qv.push(qv[i-1] + dW*dW);
    }
    return {w,qv};
  }
  function newOne(){ paths=[genPath(+nIn.value)]; draw(); }
  function many(){
    paths=[];
    for(let k=0;k<20;k++) paths.push(genPath(+nIn.value));
    draw();
  }
  function reset(){ paths=[]; draw(); }
  function draw(){
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const N=+nIn.value;
    nV.textContent=`N = ${N}`;
    // ----- top axes (path) -----
    const ymin=-3, ymax=3;
    const X = t => padL + t*(W-padL-padR);
    const Yt = v => topB - (v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*(topB-topT);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:topB,x2:W-padR,y2:topB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:topT,x2:padL,y2:topB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:Yt(0),x2:W-padR,y2:Yt(0),stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-dasharray':'2 4'}));
    // ±2√t band
    let de='', du='';
    for(let i=0;i<=100;i++){
      const t=i/100, e=2*Math.sqrt(t);
      de += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(t)+','+Yt(-e)+' ';
      du += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(t)+','+Yt(e)+' ';
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:de,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.0,'stroke-dasharray':'2 3'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:du,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.0,'stroke-dasharray':'2 3'}));
    // y ticks
    for(const v of [-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3]){
      const yp=Yt(v); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL-4,y1:yp,x2:padL,y2:yp,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:padL-6,y:yp+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=v; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // x ticks (top)
    for(const tv of [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]){
      const xp=X(tv); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:topB,x2:xp,y2:topB+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:topB+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=tv; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // top label
    const lblTop=SVG('text',{x:padL,y:topT-8,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lblTop.textContent='W_t  (with ±2√t band)'; svg.appendChild(lblTop);
    // path lines
    paths.forEach(p=>{
      const w=p.w; let d='';
      w.forEach((v,i)=>{
        const t=i/(w.length-1);

        d += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(t)+','+Yt(yc)+' ';
      });
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',

    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:botT,x2:padL,y2:botB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    // theoretical line QV = t
    let dT='';
    for(let i=0;i<=100;i++){
      const t=i/100;

    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dT,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--violet)','stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));

    for(const tv of [0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1]){
      const xp=X(tv); svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xp,y1:botB,x2:xp,y2:botB+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xp,y:botB+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=tv; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const lblBot=SVG('text',{x:padL,y:botT-8,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lblBot.textContent='running quadratic variation Σ(ΔW)²  (target: t)'; svg.appendChild(lblBot);
    // QV paths
    paths.forEach(p=>{
      const qv=p.qv; let d='';
      qv.forEach((v,i)=>{
        const t=i/(qv.length-1);

        d += (i===0?'M':'L')+X(t)+','+Yb(yc)+' ';
      });
      svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',
        stroke: paths.length>1 ? 'var(--green)' : 'var(--yellow)',
        'stroke-width': paths.length>1 ? 0.8 : 1.6,
        opacity: paths.length>1 ? 0.6 : 1}));
    });
    // legend bottom (viewport coords)
    const lg=SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:botT+14,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--violet)'});
    lg.textContent='theoretical [W]_t = t'; svg.appendChild(lg);
    // readout
    if(paths.length===1){
      const p=paths[0];
      const totQV = p.qv[p.qv.length-1];
      const totTV = p.w.slice(1).reduce((a,v,i)=>a+Math.abs(v - p.w[i]),0);
      out.textContent =
        `single path with N = ${N} steps on [0,1]\n`+
        `W_1 sample = ${p.w[N].toFixed(4)}  (target: 𝒩(0,1))\n`+
        `quadratic variation [W]_1 ≈ ${totQV.toFixed(4)}  (target: 1)\n`+
        `total variation Σ|ΔW| ≈ ${totTV.toFixed(2)}  (diverges as N→∞)`;
    } else if(paths.length>1){
      const finals = paths.map(p=>p.qv[p.qv.length-1]);
      const m = finals.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0)/finals.length;
      const sd = Math.sqrt(finals.reduce((a,b)=>a+(b-m)**2,0)/finals.length);
      out.textContent =
        `${paths.length} paths, N = ${N}\n`+
        `[W]_1 empirical mean = ${m.toFixed(4)} (target 1)\n`+
        `[W]_1 empirical std  ≈ ${sd.toFixed(4)} (target √(2/N) = ${Math.sqrt(2/N).toFixed(4)})`;
    } else {
      out.textContent='click "new path" to simulate';
    }
  }
  $('#bm-new').addEventListener('click',newOne);
  $('#bm-many').addEventListener('click',many);
  $('#bm-reset').addEventListener('click',reset);
  nIn.addEventListener('input',()=>{nV.textContent=`N = ${nIn.value}`; if(paths.length){
    if(paths.length===1) newOne(); else many();
  } else draw();});
  newOne();
})();

7. Connections

Martingales are the structural backbone of modern probability — once you see how filtrations, stopping times, and the optional-stopping theorem fit together, an enormous amount of probability theory falls into place.

Probability theory

Probability theory supplies the measure-theoretic foundation: $\sigma$-algebras as information, conditional expectation as projection. Martingales are the most-structured class of random processes that natural arguments preserve.

Stochastic calculus

Itô calculus is martingale theory in continuous time with quadratic-variation calculus on top. The Itô integral is itself a martingale; the BDG inequality gives moment bounds.

Random walks and mixing

Random walks on graphs are martingales when the graph is regular; harmonic functions on the graph are martingales of the walk, and the Markov-property machinery overlaps strongly.

Mathematical finance

The fundamental theorem of asset pricing says no-arbitrage ⇔ existence of an equivalent martingale measure. Pricing and hedging are martingale representation theorems; Girsanov implements the change of measure.

Open frontiers: backward SDEs and forward-backward systems (mathematical finance, stochastic control); rough-path theory for non-Brownian drivers; martingale optimal transport (Beiglböck, Henry-Labordère).
